Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Was not killed by Wei Yan. Surrendered Changsha of his own accord.
	There is no historical evidence to indicate he is the nasty character presented in the novel.

full_text:
Han Xuan 韓玄
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Han Xuan (韓玄). Administrator of Changsha and the superior of Huang Zhong. Han Xuan was affiliated with Liu Biao and the Shu Kingdom.
Return to theThree Kingdoms Encyclopediaor browse theEncyclopedia Directory.

Fact vs Fiction
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
Was not killed by Wei Yan. Surrendered Changsha of his own accord.
There is no historical evidence to indicate he is the nasty character presented in the novel.
Literary Appearances
Romance of the Three Kingdoms:41,53,70
Sanguozhi: Shu 2, Shu 5
Zizhi tongjian: 65
BiographySource Undefined
Han Xuan was the Grand Administrator of Changsha. He was appointed in that post before or on 209. That same year, Liu Bei marched south to take Changsha, Guiyang, Wuling, and Lingling commanderies, and Han Xuan surrendered to him. His famous subordinate, Han Zhong, surrendered with him.
